summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a session token cached once by Parse Server remains usable for authenticated writes after the backing _Session record has expired, until the auth cache TTL elapses.
expected_security_impact: The reproduction should allow an expired session token to update its user record during the stale-cache window, confirm the write with a master-key read, and then show that the same token is rejected with code 209 after the cache TTL expires.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned parse-community/parse-server at 6b1b50c8baf7e332e47307f81db7c48bca924bdc, started the target with sessionLength=1 and cacheTTL=5000, primed the auth cache, then successfully performed PUT /parse/users/:objectId with the expired token after 2.2 seconds (HTTP 200). After another 3.2 seconds, the same token failed with HTTP 400 and Parse error code 209 ("Session token is expired.").
verification_method: The exploit script checks the exact status transition, reads the user back with the master key to confirm pocTouched=true was written during the stale window, and then requires the same expired token to fail once the cache TTL has elapsed.
evidence_produced_by_run: The run log contains "stale-window update 200" for PUT /parse/users/:objectId after the session had already expired.
The run log contains a master-key read showing "pocTouched":true on the updated _User object.
The run log contains "post-ttl update 400 {\"code\":209,\"error\":\"Session token is expired.\"}" after the cache TTL elapsed.
The run log ends with "VALIDATION_SUCCESS stale cache preserved expired session access before TTL expiry" and "SUCCESS: reproduction completed and verified".

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, starts a Mongo sidecar, and mounts the checked-out tree into the verification container.
